data.title: LTIMindtree shares fall 2% after in-line Q1 results, brokerages share mixed stance
data.description:
Shares of LTIMindtree fell over 2 percent to Rs 5,078 apiece on July 18 after the company posted an in-line set of results for the April-June quarter (Q1FY26). While brokerages maintained their ratings on the stock following the Q1 numbers, they indicated limited upside from current levels.

Morgan Stanley, which has an "Equal-weight" rating with a target price of Rs 5,400, noted that the management expects to sustain both growth and margin momentum. However, the brokerage felt that the commentary could have been more bullish if macroeconomic conditions were more supportive.

Morgan Stanley added that LTIMindtree’s limited earnings per share (EPS) changes compared to peers is a positive sign.

Catch all the market action on our LIVE blog

Global brokerage HSBC maintained its "Buy" rating and raised the target price to Rs 6,000 from Rs 5,900 earlier. HSBC said the Q1 performance was broadly in line with expectations and reflected continued improvement in the company’s fundamentals.

Over the medium to long term, it expects LTIMindtree’s growth to be nearly double that of mega-cap IT companies, with scope for strong growth acceleration at still-attractive valuations.

Nuvama also reiterated its "Buy" rating and sharply increased its target price to Rs 6,200 from Rs 5,200. The brokerage believes LTIMindtree occupies a unique position — trading below the expensive, high-growth Tier-2 IT firms and above the cheaper, low-growth Tier-1 firms.

According to Nuvama, this places the company in a sweet spot for “growth at a reasonable valuation” with a favourable risk-reward profile.

On the other hand, Nomura maintained a "Neutral" rating with a target price of Rs 5,040. It highlighted improving growth visibility but added that FY26 is likely to be a “year of repair” and accounted for a slightly higher tax rate in its forecasts.

For the June quarter, LTIMindtree reported a 10.6 percent rise in net profit to Rs 1,254.1 crore. Revenue from operations increased 7.6 percent to Rs 9,840.6 crore, compared to Rs 9,142.6 crore in the same quarter of FY25.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Beijing Energy Unit Transfers Subsidy Receivables for 2 Billion Yuan ABCP Issuance
data.description:
Beijing Energy International said its unit BEIED has agreed to transfer government renewable energy subsidy receivables into a trust managed by China Industrial International Trust, according to a Thursday bourse filing.

The move forms part of an asset-backed commercial paper (ABCP) issuance worth 2 billion yuan, backed by subsidies linked to 494.6 megawatts of solar projects across China.

Shares of the company were up nearly 1% in Friday's recent trade.

Proceeds will be used mainly to repay short-term debt and fund daily operations.

data.title: CR Double-Crane Pharmaceutical to Invest 40 Million Yuan in Pharmaceutical Partnership Fund
data.description:
China Resources Double-Crane Pharmaceutical agreed to invest 40 million yuan as one of the twelve investors of a partnership fund that will invest in pharmaceuticals, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Friday.

The fund, China Resources Pharmaceutical (Chengdu) Enterprise Management Partnership (Limited Partnership), will have a capital of 1 billion yuan and a term of seven years.

The pharmaceutical company will own 4% of the fund's total capital.

data.title: HSBC Global Keeps adidas at Buy Amid Upbeat Outlook on Sporting Goods Sector
data.description:
HSBC Global Research said Thursday the sporting goods sector may be "a good place" to be amid uncertainties on a macro level.

"For once, we like both leaders adidas and Nike," analysts wrote. "The eventual return of market leader Nike will likely help the entire sector in reducing dependence on promotions/discounts. ... Brands will likely benefit from sporting events in 2026, notably with the ramp-up ahead of summer 2026 FIFA World Cup."

The research firm confirmed its buy rating on Germany's adidas, while keeping the price target at 280 euros. Fellow German sporting goods retailer Puma was rated hold with a price target of 21 euros.

Analysts added that adidas is one of the "best positioned brands" that could weather US tariff uncertainties as it has a "relatively limited" exposure to the market with a 22% share of group sales. They also noted the company's strong brand momentum and compelling product pipeline.

data.title: Yuan holds steady as firm fixing offsets dollar rebound
data.description:
China's yuan held steady despite U.S. dollar strength on Friday, as the central bank continued to guide the currency with a firm fix.

The dollar was headed for a second straight weekly gain against major peers, buoyed by solid U.S. economic data that supported the view the Federal Reserve can afford to wait a while longer before cutting interest rates again.

Prior to the market opening, the People's Bank of China set the midpoint reference rate at 7.1498 per dollar. That was slightly weaker than Thursday's fixing, which was the strongest since November last year, but was still 238 pips firmer than a Reuters' estimate.

The spot yuan is allowed to trade 2% either side of the fixed midpoint each day. Based on Friday's official guidance, the yuan is allowed to drop as far as 7.2928.

"Policymakers may be more confident after China posted solid Q2 growth of 5.2%," DBS analysts wrote in a note on Friday.

Washington's tone towards U.S.-China trade has also noticeably softened this week, with the White House now reversing its stance and allowing Nvidia to resume sales of its less advanced AI chips to China, they added.

"Allowing a firmer RMB would also mean that currency issues would be less of a bugbear in trade negotiations with the U.S."

By 0316 GMT, the yuan was little changed at 7.1806 to the dollar. It has slipped 0.2% against the dollar this month, but has appreciated 1.7% so far this year.

Its offshore counterpart traded at 7.1835 yuan per dollar , up about 0.01% in Asian trade.

The yuan is expected to fluctuate narrowly between 7.1 and 7.2 per dollar within the year amid trade uncertainties and domestic economic challenges, BCA Research's China strategist Jing Sima said in a webinar on Friday.

Key onshore vs offshore levels:

  • Overnight dollar/yuan swap onshore -18.00 pips vs. offshore -18.00

data.title: China, HK stocks set for weekly gain as Beijing's anti-price war lifts sentiment
data.description:
China and Hong Kong stocks climbed on Friday, set to finish the week higher, as Beijing's campaign against cut-throat price competition lifted investor sentiment.

** China's blue-chip CSI300 Index rose 0.5% by the lunch break, while the Shanghai Composite Index gained 0.3%. Hong Kong benchmark Hang Seng was up 0.7%.

** The CSI 300 Index has gained 1% so far this week, and on track to log a fourth straight weekly rise, while the Hang Seng Index advanced 2.2%.

** China's top leaders pledged to step up regulation of aggressive price-cutting by Chinese companies, as the world's second-biggest economy struggles to shake off persistent deflationary pressures.

** UBS analysts expect China to intensify its campaign against involution competition over the coming quarters.

** However, the path ahead may be more complex than the 2015–16 cycle, when state-owned enterprises led capacity cuts with clearer top-down coordination, the analysts said.

** China's cabinet vowed on Wednesday to rein in what it described as "irrational" competition in the electric vehicle sector, pledging to step up cost investigations and enhance price monitoring.

** EV maker Li Auto has climbed 12% so far this week, set for its biggest weekly gain since February.

** The CSI rare-earth index jumped 3.6% on Friday, with Northen Rare Earth Group up nearly 9%.

** Tech majors traded in Hong Kong rebounded more than 4% this week, partly buoyed by optimism after Nvidia said it would ramp up supply of Chinese-compliant H20 chips in the coming months and look to bring more advanced semiconductors to the world's second-largest technology market.

** China's Commerce Minister Wang Wentao said that he met with Nvidia Chief Executive Jensen Huang in Beijing on Thursday, but did not provide any details about what was discussed at their meeting.

** Shares of Alibaba rose 2.2% and were up nearly 10% this week.

data.title: Malaysia's Economy Expands 4.5% in Q2
data.description:
Malaysia’s economy expanded by 4.5% year-on-year in the second quarter of 2025, slightly up from 4.4% growth in the previous period, preliminary estimates showed.

The uptick was driven by a faster rise in agricultural activity, which grew by 2% compared to a 0.6% rise in Q1, supported by higher output in palm oil, other agriculture and livestock, and rubber sub-sectors.

Growth in services also accelerated to 5.3% from 5%, underpinned by positive performance across all sub-sectors, with wholesale & retail trade, transportation & storage, and business services being the main contributors.

On the other hand, growth moderated in manufacturing (3.8% vs 4.1%) and construction (11% vs 14.2%), while mining and quarrying output contracted further (-7.4% vs -2.7%) due to lower production in natural gas and crude oil & condensate.

On a quarterly basis, the economy contracted by 1%, following a 3.5% decline in the prior period.

data.title: SGX Sicom December RSS3 Rubber Up 1.8 Cents at 223.0 U.S. Cents/Kg
SGX Sicom Benchmark August TSR20 Rubber Up 1.7 Cents at 169.8 U.S. Cents/Kg
SGX Sicom August RSS3 Rubber Untraded at Midday
Taiwan May Sustain Healthy Exports After Front-Loading Demand Wanes — Market Talk
data.description: Taiwan's export performance could remain healthy even after front-loading demand wanes, ANZ's Bansi Madhavani says in a note. Although the record run in Taiwanese exports this year was driven partly by front-loading activity ahead of U.S. tariffs, underlying demand for semiconductor products is central to the island's export demand, the economist says. Madhavani expects Taiwan to reach some form of a trade framework or an interim arrangement with the U.S. ahead of the Aug. 1 deadline, with the original 32% reciprocal tariff negotiated down. "The key watchpoint of such an arrangement will be whether semiconductors continue to remain exempt from tariffs." (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Tesla's New Model Y L May Boost China Sales Only Marginally — Market Talk
data.description: Tesla's new six-seat Model Y L may only boost the carmaker's China sales marginally, Daiwa analysts write in a note. The company said on its Weibo account that it will release a new six-seater Model Y L this fall, but Daiwa thinks the overall dimensions of the model is smaller than many current six-seater cars in the market. Tesla is effectively fitting a six-seat configuration into a chassis traditionally designed for five seats, it says. Although that may result in compromised passenger comfort, brand loyalty could drive consumer willingness to accept this, Daiwa adds. That said, reduced legroom and relatively high pricing means the new model could only contribute to a modest recovery to Tesla's sales in China, Daiwa says. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Kangmei Pharmaceutical's Unit to Settle 44 Million Yuan in Debt
data.description:
Kangmei Pharmaceutical's unit, Chengdu Kangmei Pharmaceutical Production, agreed to settle 43.5 million yuan of debt to Guangzhou Pharmaceutical Group, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Friday.

After receiving the full payment, Guangzhou Pharma will withdraw the suit it filed at the Guangdong High People's Court against the pharmaceutical company in 2023.

Chengdu Kangmei is required to pay the settlement within 30 days.

Guangzhou Pharmaceutical is a partner of Guangdong Shennong Enterprise Management, Kangmei Pharma's controlling shareholder.

data.title: Mineral Commodities Receives Partial Payments in Norway Project Sale
data.description:
Mineral Commodities received a non-refundable payment of 1 million pounds sterling from Norge Mineraler for the sale of its Skaland Graphite project in Norway, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Norge will make available up to 410,000 pounds sterling for costs, which will not be applied against the purchase price.

The company expects to complete the deal by Oct. 31, the filing added.

data.title: Leoch International: Significant Cost Increase Due to U.S. Tariffs Weighed on Results
Leoch International Expects 1H Revenue to Increase 10%-20% on Year
Leoch International Expects 1H Net Profit to Fall 60%-80% on Year
United Cooperative Assurance Appoints Financial Advisor for Potential Merger with Arabia Insurance Cooperative
data.description:
United Cooperative Assurance (SASE:8190) appointed Estidamah Capital (SASE:1175) as its financial adviser for the proposed merger with Arabia Insurance Cooperative (SASE:8160), according to a Thursday release.

The Saudi-listed insurers signed a nonbinding memorandum of understanding on June 19 to assess the potential merger and negotiate the terms.

data.title: American Rare Earths Unit to Conduct Optimisation Tests at US Project
data.description:
American Rare Earths' US unit, Wyoming Rare, will undertake additional mineral processing optimization tests at the Cowboy State Mine, part of the Halleck Creek Rare Earths Project in the US, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The tests will evaluate multiple milling methods and optimise the beneficiation circuit, the filing said.

The company also plans to begin test mining in the near term to extract feedstock, the filing added.

Shares of the company fell past 1% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Gaush Meditech Unit Gains Guangdong Nod for Contact Lens; Shares Rise 4%
data.description:
Gaush Meditech said its subsidiary Gaush Tech has received a medical device registration certificate from the Guangdong Medical Products Administration for its corneal contact lens, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

Shares of the company were up about 4% in Friday morning trade.

The approved lens assists in the scanning of eye tissues when used with the appropriate diagnostic equipment.

The product is designed to help diagnose eye diseases, according to the filing.

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: Upbeat Corporate Earnings Boost Optimism
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

No major economic events expected; trading updates from Telenor, SKF, Burberry, Telia, Danske Bank

Opening Call:

European stock futures traded higher early Friday. Asian stock benchmarks were mostly higher; the dollar inched lower; Treasury yields were down; while oil futures and gold weakened.

Equities:

Stock futures were up, as investors continue to look to corporate earnings for cues. Upbeat earnings from some major U.S. companies and strong U.S. retail sales data have lifted optimism that markets can weather tariffs and shifting trade policy.

"Relative to expectations around Liberation Day, tariffs may have a more idiosyncratic impact, depending on the country and sector, given their piecemeal application, delayed starts and potential deals," said Morgan Stanley investment strategist Monica Guerra.

Forex:

The U.S. dollar shuffled between gains and losses amid mixed signals. On the one hand, stronger-than-expected U.S. economic data released overnight could dent rate-cut expectations, thereby supporting the greenback.

However, "USD remains vulnerable to the downside if concerns about U.S. policymaking further undermine investor confidence in USD assets," CBA's Carol Kong said. The dollar's weakness earlier this week due to worries over Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's possible dismissal by President Trump is a case in point, she said.

Bonds:

Treasury yields are holding at levels near key numbers, including 4.5% for the 10-year, and 5% for the 30-year, suggesting that tariffs are only having a mild impact on asset prices, strategists said. But that could change if inflation sets in more durably.

"The Fed will try to see this as transitory," said John Briggs, head of U.S. rates strategy at Natixis Corporate and Investment Banking.

Fed Gov. Christopher Waller called for the central bank to cut interest rates at its July meeting, breaking ranks with Powell. "The risks to the economy are weighted toward cutting sooner rather than later," Waller said, as President Trump intensifies pressure on the Fed to lower borrowing costs.

Energy:

Oil futures consolidated amid mixed signals. On the demand side, economic resilience in the U.S. and China has provided some support, Tickmill's Joseph Dahrieh said.

However, this has been tempered by large increases in U.S. gasoline and diesel stockpiles, casting doubt on the strength of fuel demand as the peak summer travel season progresses, he said.

Metals:

Gold edged higher on possible position adjustments amid a lack of fresh catalysts. However, price gains may be curbed by overnight U.S. data, which showed economic resilience that could damp Fed rate-cut expectations.

-

Iron ore strengthened, buoyed by investors' optimism about improving steel mill margins amid China's efforts to remove overcapacity, and prospects for fresh stimulus for the property sector, ANZ Research said.

The policy that will have the best chance of stabilizing the Chinese property sector is the government buying up excess housing inventory, which can improve the developers' credit health and ease their financial stress, CBA's Vivek Dhar said.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

The Global Risks That Come With the Loss of an Independent Fed

President Trump's threat to attempt to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has raised a pressing but potentially unanswerable question: What would the global economy and financial markets look like without an independent U.S. central bank?

Shielded from White House interference, the independent Fed has increasingly served as an anchor for U.S. and global markets, stepping in to steady the ship during the 2008-09 financial crisis, the Covid pandemic and other shocks of recent years. Economists credit the central bank's ability to help keep things stable in large part to its power to make moves it deems necessary, regardless of politics.

Bonds Breathe a Sign of Relief at Mild Tariff Data. What Could Change That.

A whiff of tariff inflation showed up in economic data this week, providing the first clues of how tariff policy might impact prices and the economy.

Signals from the bond market suggest tariffs are only having a mild impact on asset prices, strategists say. But that could change if inflation sets in more durably.

German Startup Wants to Regrow Europe's 'Spine' With AI Fighter Pilots, Drone Walls

MUNICH-Inside an unassuming redbrick building tucked beside railroad tracks in the Bavarian capital, engineers behind doors marked "maximum security" toil on drone prototypes, flight simulators and lines of code they believe will shape the next era of warfare.

Helsing, a 4-year-old artificial-intelligence defense startup, has quietly emerged as one of Europe's fastest-growing companies-and a poster child for a continent urgently re-engineering its defense in an era of fracturing alliances.

BHP Flags Higher Costs, Delays on Potash Project; May Sell Nickel Arm

Mining giant BHP Group flagged rising costs and delays at a giant potash project in Canada and said it may sell its Australian nickel operations, as it reported record annual iron ore and copper production.

Australia-based BHP, the world's biggest miner by market value, has been reshaping its business to bet on an accelerating energy transition and global population growth via investments in copper and potash, a fertilizer ingredient. The miner currently relies on the steel ingredient iron ore for more than half its earnings.

Netflix Raises Revenue, Margin Forecasts After Strong Second Quarter

Netflix raised its revenue and operating margin forecasts for the year, continuing a strong run that has propelled its shares to new highs.

The streaming service said member additions, price increases and its growing ad business drove revenue in the second quarter.

Boeing to Sell Up to 18 Dreamliner Jets to Gulf Air

Boeing plans to sell up to 18 of its 787 Dreamliner jets to Gulf Air.

The jet maker said Thursday it has agreed to sell 12 of the jets, with options for six more.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: China June aluminium imports jump 24.1% versus last year
data.description:
China's imports of unwrought aluminium and products jumped 24.1% year-on-year in June, customs data showed on Friday.

The world's top consumer of the light metal imported 300,000 metric tons of unwrought aluminium and products last month, data from the General Administration of Customs showed.

The data includes primary metal and unwrought, alloyed aluminium.

In the first half of 2025, China imported a total of 1.98 million tons of unwrought aluminium and products, down 3.2% from the corresponding 2024 period.

Imports of bauxite, a key raw material for aluminium, jumped 36.2% on the year to 18.12 million tons in June, driven by higher domestic output of aluminium.

Imports from January to June totalled 103.25 million tons, up 33.6% year-on-year.

data.title: Mapletree Logistics Trust Completes Divestment of Malaysian Property; Shares Up Nearly 1%
data.description:
Mapletree Logistics Trust completed the divestment of Subang 2 in Malaysia, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Shares of the trust were up nearly 1% in recent trading.

Following the divestment, the trust's portfolio stands at 176 properties with total value of assets under management at SG$13.2 billion.

data.title: RBA's Shock On-Hold Rates Call Prompts Soul Searching — Market Talk
data.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia's shock decision to keep interest rates on hold this month has prompted a lot of soul searching among economists. Luci Ellis, chief economist at Westpac, has been open with the way she is processing the surprise. The last thing a central bank wants is to be seen as not independent, including from the markets, she says. Cutting the cash rate in July, rather than the RBA's original preference for a cautious and predictable quarterly pace, coinciding with fresh forecasts, would have looked a bit like the market pricing had forced the cut, she adds. There will be more RBA communication next week for economists to ponder. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

data.title: Telechoice International Exits Singapore Exchange's Watch-List; Shares Surge 12%
data.description:
Telechoice International received in-principle approval from the Singapore Exchange Securities Trading for its application to exit from the Watch-List, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Shares of the company surged 12% in recent trading.

The company was removed from the Watch-List, effective July 18.

data.title: Attendo Kicks Off SEK150 Million Share Buyback
data.description:
Swedish health and care services group Attendo will initiate a share repurchase program worth 150 million kronor on Friday.

The program, set to run until Oct. 23, will be carried out by Skandinaviska Enskilda Banken on Nasdaq Stockholm, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Arta TechFin to Sell Arta Global Markets Subsidiary for HK$6.1 Million; Shares Gain 7%
data.description:
Arta TechFin has agreed to sell its Arta Global Markets subsidiary for HK$6.1 million, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

The financial firm's shares were up over 7% in morning trade on Friday.

Arta TechFin is selling the business to Lau Fu Wing, a director of Arta Global Markets as well as Arta Global Futures, another Arta TechFin subsidiary.

Arta TechFin said the sale would help streamline its business operations.

data.title: Polycab India shares rise nearly 3% on strong Q1; UBS ups target price
data.description:
Shares of Polycab India rallied nearly 3 percent to Rs 7,065 per share in morning trade on July 18 after Citi Research and UBS dished out bullish calls on the stock following its first quarter earnings.

Net profit for the quarter increased by 50 percent from the year-ago quarter to Rs 600 crore, while revenue from operations for the quarter increased by 25.7 percent to Rs 5,906 crore.

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

Polycab's Earnings Before Interest, Tax, Depreciation and Amortisation (EBITDA) increased by 47.1 percent to Rs 858 crore. EBITDA margins for the quarter expanded by over 200 basis points from the year-ago period to 14.5 percent.

Following the development, Citi Research maintained a Buy rating with a target price of Rs 7,700 per share, implying an upside potential of 12 percent from the last close on the NSE. The wires and cables segment continued to lead growth, with the FMEG (fast-moving electrical goods) division also showing strength, posting 18 percent revenue growth and a 283 bps expansion in margin to 2.1 percent. Notably, the FMEG business turned in its second consecutive profitable quarter.

Also read: Trading Plan: Can Nifty 50, Bank Nifty manage to rebound amid rangebound trading?

UBS also reiterated a Buy rating on Polycab, raising its target price to Rs 8,100 per share. The new target forecasts an upside of 18 percent. The brokerage highlighted a robust start to the fiscal year and suggested that both the cables and wires (C&W) and FMEG segments delivered strong performances.

Polycab’s wires and cables segment posted a robust performance, with revenue rising to Rs 5,228 crore in the June quarter, up from Rs 3,995 crore a year ago. The FMEG (fast-moving electrical goods) business also saw healthy growth, with revenue increasing to Rs 454 crore from Rs 385 crore in the same period last year. Meanwhile, revenue from the EPC (engineering, procurement, and construction) segment declined to Rs 347 crore from Rs 427 crore a year ago.

Notably, the EPC division, previously clubbed under the ‘others’ segment, will now be reported separately after meeting the qualitative criteria for distinct disclosure.

At about 9:40 am, shares of the company were trading at Rs 7,050, higher by 2.5 percent from the last close on the NSE. Polycab India shares have risen 14 percent in the last month but still trade 5 percent lower on a year-to-date basis.
